pi_items.source: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis official website and web search

pi_items.verified_value: UNRATE (Unemployment Rate) and CIVPART (Labor Force Participation Rate) are real, standard FRED series
Unemployment Rate - the number of unemployed as a percentage of the labor force, from U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics
Civilian Labor Force Participation Rate - the number of people in the labor force as a percentage of the civilian noninstitutional population
Both UNRATE and CIVPART have continuous data from January 1948 to present (February 2026)
pi_items.source: FRED series documentation and website fetch

pi_items.verified_value: Data available from January 1948 to February 2026 (last update March 6, 2026)
Data available from January 1948 to February 2026 (last update March 6, 2026)
FRED provides CSV download capability through the graph interface
FRED provides PNG image export of graphs through the export options menu
Standard Firefox bookmark title capability - users can bookmark pages with custom titles

pi_items.metadata_value: capability to graph both UNRATE and CIVPART on same chart
pi_items.verified_value: FRED allows adding multiple data series to a single graph using the EDIT GRAPH button
Historical unemployment rate: average 5.8% (1948-2020), peak 10% (Oct 2009), recent 3.64-5.35% (2021-2026), current 4.4% (Feb 2026)
Historical labor force participation rate: peak ~67% (late 1990s-early 2000s), recent low 60s, current 62.0% (Feb 2026)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real, publicly available data from the Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) database maintained by the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis. Both required series are genuine government economic indicators: UNRATE (Unemployment Rate) and CIVPART (Civilian Labor Force Participation Rate) are published by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. Data for both series is continuously available from January 1948 to February 2026 (last updated March 6, 2026). Current values are UNRATE 4.4% and CIVPART 62.0%. The FRED website at fred.stlouisfed.org supports creating custom multi-series graphs, downloading data as CSV files, exporting graphs as PNG images, and browser bookmarking capabilities required by the task. The expected data value ranges (UNRATE 2.5-15%, CIVPART 55-70%) align with historical economic data patterns.
